questions.question: For the live similarity-search component, what reasoning favored the incremental-insert graph design over the cluster-rebuild layout, and what cost triggered it?
questions.gold_answer: The graph-navigation index was chosen over the cluster/centroid (IVF) approach because rebuild cost spiked 3x at the 50M-vector shard boundary and re-clustering on corpus growth is a non-starter for a live index, whereas the graph approach supports incremental inserts.

questions.question: How much did a two-stage scoring approach improve ranked-result relevance compared with using one model to fetch candidates?
questions.gold_answer: Cross-encoder reranking raised nDCG@10 by 0.09 over bi-encoder-only

questions.question: What was the storage saving from a lower-precision representation, and did answer quality drop noticeably?
questions.gold_answer: int8 quantization cut memory 3.8x with under 1% recall loss at k=10

questions.question: On rare or unusual searches, how did mixing keyword and semantic matching compare to a purely neural approach?
questions.gold_answer: BM25 + dense fusion beat dense-only by 11 MRR@10 points on long-tail queries

questions.question: What efficiency gain came from processing many items at once during the encoding stage rather than one request at a time?
questions.gold_answer: Batch size 64 cut per-document GPU cost 2.3x compared to one-at-a-time inference

questions.question: Did changing the scoring math used during candidate lookup reorder the results, and was anything faster?
questions.gold_answer: Dot-product on normalized vectors cut query latency 18% with no ranking change versus cosine

questions.question: How did adding records to the store on the fly stack up against periodic from-scratch builds on both result quality and turnaround time?
questions.gold_answer: Incremental upsert kept 98% of recall vs full rebuild and was 6x faster to complete

questions.question: What was the retrieval gain from training the model on the in-house corpus versus shipping a generic off-the-shelf release?
questions.gold_answer: Domain fine-tuning improved recall@5 by 14 percentage points over the base checkpoint

questions.question: How much did storing precomputed vectors for the most-run searches lighten the load on the encoding service?
questions.gold_answer: Embedding cache for top-1000 queries cut embedding service load by 34%

questions.question: What speed gain came from giving each account its own slice of data instead of pooling everyone together?
questions.gold_answer: Per-tenant sharding lowered P95 query latency 27% compared to the shared index

questions.question: Did using a thinned-out computation pattern in the second-pass model cost any accuracy, and how did its speed change?
questions.gold_answer: Sparse attention in the reranker matched dense quality and was 2.1x faster

questions.question: What coverage improvement came from automatically rewriting what users typed before fetching, and what speed penalty did it carry?
questions.gold_answer: Learned query expansion boosted recall@20 by 8 points at a cost of 55ms added median latency

questions.question: What language was the document intake service originally written in before the team switched runtimes due to resource exhaustion under load?
questions.gold_answer: Python

questions.question: Which third-party vector database did the Search team trial and ultimately turn down before building their own persistence layer?
questions.gold_answer: Weaviate

questions.question: What processing model for generating vector representations was replaced because it caused the document intake pipeline to stall during a load spike?
questions.gold_answer: Synchronous (inline) embedding during ingestion; replaced with an asynchronous queue-based embedding architecture.

questions.question: Which query language did the team trial for the customer-facing console backend before reverting to a more conventional interface style?
questions.gold_answer: GraphQL

questions.question: Which augmentation technique that synthesizes fictitious relevant passages was prototyped and then dropped from the production serving path?
questions.gold_answer: HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings / LLM-generated hypothetical answers for query expansion)

questions.question: Which managed versus self-hosted tradeoff drove the selection of the primary embedding storage backend?
questions.gold_answer: Qdrant was chosen over Pinecone because self-hosting requirements and cost predictability at scale favored it.

questions.question: What library and pretrained checkpoint did the team standardize on for converting text to dense vectors?
questions.gold_answer: sentence-transformers with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 was standardized as the default encoder.

questions.question: Why did the team buy an outside service for reordering search hits rather than operating their own ranking model in-house?
questions.gold_answer: Cohere Rerank API was chosen to avoid the overhead of managing a self-hosted GPU fleet for cross-encoder inference.

questions.question: What durable queue was placed between the page-fetching stage and the components that compute dense representations, and why over the alternative?
questions.gold_answer: Apache Kafka was chosen as the durable event stream between the document crawler and embedding workers, because it preserves replay semantics that SQS lacked.

questions.question: What persistence solution did the team choose for recording per-request timing metrics instead of a purpose-built observability store?
questions.gold_answer: TimescaleDB (a PostgreSQL extension) was chosen for query-level latency telemetry instead of a dedicated time-series database.

questions.question: Which framework powers the testing harness, and what made it preferable over options needing hand-labeled ground truth?
questions.gold_answer: Ragas was chosen for the eval harness because it computes faithfulness and answer relevancy without requiring gold labels.

questions.question: Which UI framework did the customer-facing console adopt, and what fetch-pattern fit pushed the team off the more popular option?
questions.gold_answer: Remix was chosen over Next.js because its nested data-loading model better matched the server-side query patterns needed by the web dashboard.

questions.question: What outside payments platform handles subscription and consumption charges, and what risk concern steered the team away from building it themselves?
questions.gold_answer: Stripe with usage-based metering was chosen over a homegrown invoicing system primarily to reduce compliance (PCI/revenue recognition) surface area.

questions.question: What shared data store was introduced to make request throttling survive rollouts, and which structure inside it backs the per-customer caps?
questions.gold_answer: Redis sorted sets were adopted for per-tenant rate limiting after the in-process token bucket was found to lose state on pod restarts.

questions.question: Which open instrumentation standard did the team migrate to, and which span store replaced the costly proprietary agent they ran before?
questions.gold_answer: OpenTelemetry was adopted for instrumentation with Grafana Tempo as the trace backend, replacing custom Datadog APM agents.

questions.question: What caused the ranking service to go down in mid-2024 and how did the team resolve it?
questions.gold_answer: Memory leak in the cross-encoder tokenizer's batch processing; fixed by capping batch size to 32 and adding explicit cleanup after each request.

questions.question: Why did customers lose access to paid tiers overnight in late 2024 and what was the immediate fix?
questions.gold_answer: Stripe webhook signing secret was rotated without updating HELIX's secrets store; all renewal events were rejected. Fix was syncing the new secret into the secrets store.

questions.question: Why did the early-2025 data import fail in a way where records looked successfully saved but carried no usable meaning for retrieval?
questions.gold_answer: The object store returned HTTP 200 with empty bodies during maintenance, which the pipeline misread as valid empty documents instead of errors, producing empty embedding vectors.

questions.question: What caused partial corruption of the search index after the network event in autumn 2024?
questions.gold_answer: Asymmetric network partition healing caused dual-leader split-brain on four shards, producing duplicate write acknowledgments and corrupted index segments.

questions.question: When did one client's private search hits get exposed to unrelated accounts, and what was the underlying technical mistake?
questions.gold_answer: During a blue-green deployment in Q2'24, the query router config pointed all tenants' queries to one tenant's dedicated index pool for 22 minutes, exposing results across tenants.

questions.question: What was behind the silent accuracy drop in the vector-generation service in early 2025 that surfaced only under heavy traffic?
questions.gold_answer: A missing per-thread CUDA context initialization caused workers to share a single context, producing race conditions and NaN embedding vectors under concurrent load.

questions.question: How did the team discover that quality benchmarks had been giving false-green results for a month and a half in mid-2024?
questions.gold_answer: A cron job refreshing the golden eval dataset had been failing silently due to expired credentials for six weeks; benchmarks ran against stale data and missed real regressions.

questions.question: Why were European users unable to log into the product for several hours in autumn 2024?
questions.gold_answer: A CDN edge rule mis-routed EU users' authenticated traffic to the US origin, where their session tokens were rejected because JWT signing keys are scoped per region.

questions.question: Why did response times slowly worsen on the largest customers' data partitions in spring 2025 with no software release involved?
questions.gold_answer: The compaction job inherited a stale systemd fd limit, causing it to abort silently on shards over 40GB; unmerged tombstones accumulated, inflating per-query scan cost.

questions.question: What broke processing of non-English files in summer 2024 right after a routine library-cleanup change?
questions.gold_answer: A PDF parsing library was unpinned and auto-upgraded; the newer version changed text extraction encoding from UTF-8 to Latin-1, crashing the embedding service on non-ASCII characters.

questions.question: How many documents at a time does the embedding worker process in a single forward pass?
What build-time exploration factor is used when constructing the approximate nearest-neighbor graph?
At what daily request volume does a non-paying customer start receiving rate-limit errors?
questions.gold_answer: 10,000 API calls per day

questions.question: How many sub-quantizer segments are used in the compressed representation of stored vectors?
Who is currently on the hook for waking up to production pages across the entire stack?
questions.gold_answer: A unified SRE rotation covering all components (Search, Platform, Growth, Billing)

questions.question: Which group ended up responsible for the cross-encoder scoring layer after it was pulled out of the retrieval stack?
questions.gold_answer: Platform team

questions.question: After the move to a wider vector representation, what corpus-wide rebuild did the storage tier have to perform?
questions.gold_answer: A full re-index of the vector store was required.

questions.question: Who authored the commit that triggered the dropped-records outage, and had that same engineer also designed the intake subsystem originally?
questions.gold_answer: Ben made the change that caused the incident; Ana originally architected the ingestion pipeline — they are different people.

questions.question: What was the first live experiment run through the routing layer's split-testing mechanism, and did it become permanent?
questions.gold_answer: The synonym expansion experiment (Growth team) was the first live A/B experiment; it became the default after a 9% CTR lift.

questions.question: How did the assessment framework's coverage change once the re-ranking stage joined the pipeline?
questions.gold_answer: The eval harness was extended from retrieval-precision-only to also include end-to-end answer faithfulness judged by an LLM.

questions.question: After the shift to usage-based pricing, which two internal services had to start emitting metered events?
questions.gold_answer: The ingestion pipeline and query router both required new metering instrumentation to support consumption-based billing.

questions.question: Did the graph-based approximate nearest-neighbor structure stay in service across every customer tier following the slowdown early in 2025?
questions.gold_answer: No — high-cardinality tenants were migrated to IVF-PQ after the latency regression; HNSW was not retained for that segment.

questions.question: When the customer-facing surface added its usage-inspection view, did the team stand up fresh data plumbing or reuse a store already running for operations?
questions.gold_answer: They repurposed an existing ClickHouse table that Platform had already provisioned for SRE use — no new data infrastructure was built.
